subject: Project Management
title: Crystal Methods
description: A quick win: understand Crystal Methods.

hook.text: Alistair Cockburn studied dozens of successful software teams. They had almost nothing in common.

buildup.text: Different team sizes needed different processes. One size could never fit all.

discovery.text: He created Crystal — a family of methods scaled by team size and project criticality.

twist.text: Crystal never caught on like Scrum or Kanban. It was too nuanced for mass-market adoption.

climax.text: Its core insight survived: process should fit the team, not the other way around.

punchline.text: The right answer was 'it depends.' Nobody wanted to hear that.

quiz.question: What was Crystal's core insight about software methodology?
options: Process should be scaled to team size and project criticality
All teams should follow the same process
Documentation is more important than code
